headline: Terengganu Turtle Nesting Sites Decline 62% Since 2000
context: The Department of Fisheries recorded only 3,400 turtle nesting events across Terengganu in 2024, down 62% from 8,900 in 2000, with leatherback turtles functionally extinct.

cards.big: Rantau Abang was once the world's largest leatherback nesting beach.
cards.sub: Zero leatherback nests have been recorded since 2018. The species is functionally extinct in Malaysia.

cards.big: Turtle egg consumption remains legal in Terengganu, Sabah, and Sarawak.
cards.sub: An estimated 2.1 million eggs are consumed annually. Federal protection does not override state prerogatives.

cards.big: Artificial lighting on 34 beachfront resorts disorients hatchlings.
cards.sub: Studies show 78% of hatchlings on lit beaches move inland instead of toward the sea.

cards.big: Turtle-based tourism at Perhentian and Redang generates RM85M annually.
cards.sub: Economic value of live turtles for tourism far exceeds the RM12M annual egg trade.

cards.big: We are eating RM12M in eggs while destroying RM85M in tourism.
Turtle conservation requires a federal ban on egg collection with compensation to state governments for lost revenue. The constitutional obstacle is real but the current trajectory is extinction.
